questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A semicircle is drawn outwardly on chord $A B$ of the circle with centre $O$ and unit radius. The perpendicular from $O$ to $A B$ meets the semicircle on $A B$ at $C$. Then length of $A B$ so that $O C$ has maximum length is $\sqrt{\mathrm{K}}$. Then K is equal to___
questions.solution:
Let $O D=\sqrt{\mathrm{a}}$ and $A D=\sqrt{1-\mathrm{a}}$ $O C=O D+C D=\sqrt{1-a}$
$O C^2=(\sqrt{a}+\sqrt{1-a})^2=1+2 \sqrt{a(1-a}$
$a(1-a)$ should be maximum
$\Rightarrow$ at $\mathrm{a}=\frac{1}{2}$, the above expression attains maximum value
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \mathrm{OD}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \\ & \therefore \mathrm{AD}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\end{aligned}$
$A B=2 A D=\sqrt{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $(x, y)$ be satisfy the curve $x^2+y^2-6 x-8 y+21=0$ and $m_1$ and $m_2$ are minimum and maximum values of $\left(\frac{x}{y}\right)$. If $m_1^2+m_2^2=\frac{p}{q}$ where $p, q \in N$, then find the least value of $(p+q)$.
questions.solution:
$$ \frac{x}{y}=\frac{1}{m} $$ Let $y=m x$ be the tangent Applying, $$ \begin{aligned} & p=r \\ &\left|\frac{3 m-4}{\sqrt{1+m^2}}\right|=2 \\ & 9 m^2-24 m+16=4+4 m^2 \\ & 5 m^2-24 m+12=0 \\ & 12 m^2-24 m+5=0 \\ & \frac{m_1}{m_1} \end{aligned} $$
$\begin{aligned}\left(m_1+m_2\right)^2-2 m_1 m_2 & =4-2\left(\frac{5}{12}\right) \Rightarrow \frac{19}{6} \equiv \frac{p}{q} \\ \therefore \quad p+q & =25\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let two parallel lines $L_1$ and $L_2$ with positive slope are tangent to the circle $C_1: x^2+y^2-2 x-16 y+64=0$. If $L_1$ is also tangent to the circle $C_2: x^2+y^2-2 x+2 y-2=0$ and equation of $L_2$ is $a \sqrt{a} x-b y+c-a \sqrt{a}=0$ where $a, b, c \in N$, then find the value of $(a+b+c)$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & C_1 \Rightarrow \text { centre }(1,8), \text { radius } \Rightarrow 1 \\ & C_2 \Rightarrow \text { centre }(-1,1), \text { radius } \Rightarrow 2\end{aligned}$ Line $L_1$ is tranverse common tangent of $C_1$ and $C_2$ then it cut $\mathrm{O}_1, \mathrm{O}_2$ internally in the ratio of $1: 2$ at point P then $\begin{aligned} & P \Rightarrow\left(\frac{1+2}{1+2}, \frac{-1+16}{1+2}\right) \\ & P \Rightarrow(1,5)\end{aligned}$ Equation of $L_1$ with positive slope is $(y-5)=m(x-1)$
$m x-y-m+5=0$ From $\mathrm{O}_2$, we have $\left|\frac{\mathrm{m}+1-\mathrm{m}+5}{\sqrt{\mathrm{~m}^2+1}}\right|=2$ $\mathrm{m}=2 \sqrt{2}$ $2 \sqrt{2} x-y-2 \sqrt{2}+5=0$ Equation of $\mathrm{L}_2 \Rightarrow 2 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{y}+\mathrm{k}=0$ $\left|\frac{\mathrm{k}+2 \sqrt{2}-5}{3}\right|=2$ [distance between parallel lines] $k= \pm 6+5-2 \sqrt{2}$ Equation of $L_2$ with ${+ve}$ $\quad$ $y$-intercept $\begin{aligned} & 2 \sqrt{2} x-y+11-2 \sqrt{2}=0 \\ & \text { then } a=2, b=1, c=11 \\ & a+b+c=14\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A circle $x^2+y^2+4 x-2 \sqrt{2} y+c=0$ is the director circle of circle $S_1$ and $S_1$ is the director circle of circle $S_2$ and so on. If the sum of radii of all these circles is 2 , then the value of $c$ is equal to $\sqrt{\mathrm{n}}$ where $\mathrm{n} \in \mathrm{N}$. Find the value of $n$.
questions.solution:
Radius of given circle $=\sqrt{4+2-\mathrm{c}}=\sqrt{6-\mathrm{c}}=\mathrm{a}$ (say)
Now radius of circle $S_1=\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}$, radius of circle $S_2=\frac{a}{2}$ and so on.
Now $\mathrm{a}+\frac{\mathrm{a}}{\sqrt{2}}+\frac{\mathrm{a}}{2}+\ldots . . \infty=2$ (Given) $\Rightarrow \mathrm{a}\left(\frac{1}{1-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}}\right)=2 \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{a} \sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{2}-1}=2$
$\Rightarrow a=2-\sqrt{2}=\sqrt{6-c} \Rightarrow 4+2-4 \sqrt{2}=6-c . \quad$ 
Hence $c=4 \sqrt{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If a circle of radius 2 unit touches the $y$-axis at the origin, ' $O$ ' and intersects the lines $y=(2-\sqrt{3}) x$ and $y=-(2+\sqrt{3}) x$ in the I and IV quadrants at $A$ and $B$ respectively, then area of $\triangle A O B$ (in square units) is:
questions.solution: $\operatorname{Ar} .(\triangle A O B)=\operatorname{Ar} .(\triangle A O C)+\operatorname{Ar}(\triangle C O B)=\frac{1}{2} \times 2 \times 2 \times \sin 150^{\circ}+\frac{1}{2} \times 2 \times 2 \times \sin 30^{\circ}=2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $S$ be a circle of radius 1 with centre at $A$. Two circles with radius $r$ and $R$ and centre at $C$ and $D$ are externally tangent to each other and internally tangent to $S$. If $\angle D A C=120^{\circ}$, find the value of $R r+3 R+3 r$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & & \cos 120^{\circ} & =\frac{(1-r)^2+(1-R)^2-(r+R)^2}{2(1-r)(1-R)}=-\frac{1}{2} \\ \Rightarrow & & 3-3 R-3 r-r R & =0 \\ \Rightarrow & & R r+3 R+3 r & =3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider a series of $n$ concentric circles $C_1, C_2, \ldots \ldots, C_n$ with radii $r_1, r_2, r_3, \ldots \ldots, r_n$ respectively satisfying $r_1>r_2>r_3>\ldots \ldots .>r_n$ and $r_1=10$
The circles are such that the chord of contact of tangents from any point on $C_i$ to $C_{i+1}$ is a tangent to $C_{i+2}$ where $i=1,2,3, \ldots \ldots$.
Find the value of $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_{r=1}^n r_i$, if the angle between the tangents from any point of $C_1$ to $C_2$ is $60^{\circ}$.
questions.solution: $$ x^2+y^2=r_1^2 $$ Chord of contact : $x x_1+y y_1=r_2^2$ $$ \begin{aligned} & p=r \\ & \Rightarrow \quad\left|\frac{r_2^2}{\sqrt{x_1^2+y_1^2}}\right|=r_3 \Rightarrow\left|\frac{r_2^2}{r_1}\right|=r_3 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad r_2^2=r_1 r_3 \end{aligned} $$ Hence, $r_1, r_2, r_3, \ldots$. G.P. $$ \cos 60^{\circ}=\frac{r_2}{10}=\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow r_2=5 $$ Hence, G.P. is $10,5, \frac{5}{2}, \ldots \ldots$. $$ \therefore \quad \lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_{r=1}^n r_i=\frac{10}{1-\frac{1}{2}}=20 $$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two parallel chords of a circle $S$ have length 10 and 14 and are 6 units apart. If a regular polygon of 12 sides is inscribed in a circle $S$, then find the area of regular polygon.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} p_1+p_2 & =6 \\ \sqrt{r^2-25}+\sqrt{r^2-49} & =6 \\ r^2 & =50 \\ A & =n\left(\frac{1}{2} r^2 \sin \frac{2 \pi}{n}\right)=12 \times \frac{50}{2} \sin \frac{\pi}{6}=150\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $x, y, z$ and $t$ be real number such that $(x, y)$ lies on a circle having radius $3 ;(z, t)$ lies on a circle having radius 2 and $x t-y z=6$. Find the greatest value of $P=x z$.
[Note: Both circles have centre at origin.]
questions.solution:
$x^2+y^2=9$ ...(1)
$z^2+t^2=4$ ...(2)
Put
$$
\begin{aligned}
x & =3 \cos \theta \text { and } z=2 \cos \alpha \\
y & =3 \sin \theta \text { and } t=2 \sin \alpha \\
x t-y z & =6 \\
6(\sin \alpha \cos \theta-\cos \alpha \sin \theta) & =6 \\
\sin (\alpha-\theta) & =1 \\
p & =x z=6 \cos \theta \cos \theta \\
p & =3[\cos (\theta+\alpha)+\underbrace{\cos (\theta-\alpha)}_0] \\
p & =3 \cos (\theta+\alpha) \\
p_{\max } & =3
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider two circles of radii $r_1$ and $r_2$ passing through vertex $A$ of $\triangle A B C$ and touching side $B C$ at points $B$ and $C$ respectively. If $a=5$ and $\angle A=30^{\circ}$, then $\sqrt{r_1 r_2}$ is equal to:
questions.solution: In $\triangle A C_1 C_2$ $\cos A=\frac{r_1^2+r_2^2-\left(C_1 C_2\right)^2}{2 r_1 r_2}=\cos 60^{\circ}$ $r_1^2+r_2^2-\left(C_1 C_2\right)^2=r_1 r_2$ $\Rightarrow \quad r_1 r_2=25$ ...(1) $\Rightarrow \quad r_1 r_2=25$ ...
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let the circle $S: x^2+y^2-a x-b y+c=0$ intersects the pair of straight lines $x y-4 x-3 y+12=0$ orthogonally and the circle lies in the first quadrant. If $S$ touches the circle $S_1:(x-3)^2+y^2=r^2, r \in N$, then find the sum of all possible values of $r$.
questions.solution: $$ \begin{aligned} & x y-3 x-4 y+12=0 \\ & (x-3)(y-4)=0 \Rightarrow x=3 \text { and } y=4 \\ & S: x^2+y^2-6 x-8 y+c=0 \end{aligned} $$ For $R=3, r=1$ and 7 For $R=2, r=2$ and 6 For $R=1, r=3$ and 5 For $R=0, r=4$ $\therefore$ Sum of all possible values of $$ r=1+2+3+4+5+6+7=28 $$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
In $\triangle A B C$, let $|a-5|+(7 \cos B-3)^2+\log _3(1+|c-7|)=0$. A circle drawn with the altitude $A D$ as diameter which meets the sides $A B$ and $A C$ at $E$ and $F$ respectively.
If $(B E)(C F)=\frac{p}{q \sqrt{11}}$ where $p, q \in N$, then find the least value of $(p+q)$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} a & =5, \cos B=\frac{3}{7}, c=7 \\ B E & =3 \cos B=\frac{9}{7} \\ C F & =2 \cos C=2 \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{11}} \\ \therefore \quad(B E)(C F) & =\frac{18}{7 \sqrt{11}} \equiv \frac{p}{q \sqrt{11}} \\ \therefore \quad p+q & =25\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $A B C$ be a triangle with $\angle B=45^{\circ}$ and $c=5$. A circle with center $A$ and radius $A B$ meets $B C$ at $D$. If altitude from vertex $A$ to the side $B C$ meet the circle at $E$ then area of $\triangle B E D$ is $\frac{p}{q}(\sqrt{q}-1)$. Find the value of $(p+q)$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} B P & =\frac{5}{\sqrt{2}}=P D \\ P E & =5-\frac{5}{\sqrt{2}} \\ \therefore \operatorname{Ar}(\triangle B E D) & =\frac{1}{2} \times 2 \times \frac{5}{\sqrt{2}}\left(5-\frac{5}{\sqrt{2}}\right) \\ & =\frac{25}{2}(\sqrt{2}-1) \\ \Rightarrow \quad p+q & =25+2=27\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If sum of reciprocal of radii of all the circles which touches all the lines represented by the equation $x^2 y-2 x y^2-4 x y=0$ is $k\left(1+\cos \frac{2 \pi}{p}\right)$, where $k, p \in N$, then find the value of $(k+p)$
questions.solution: $\begin{gathered}x^2 y-2 x y^2-4 x y=0 \Rightarrow x y(x-2 y-4)=0 \\ r=\frac{\Delta}{s}=\frac{\frac{1}{2} \times 2 \times 4}{3+\sqrt{5}}=\frac{4}{3+\sqrt{5}} \\ \text { Required sum }=\frac{2}{r}=\frac{2(3+\sqrt{5})}{4}=\frac{6+2 \sqrt{5}}{4}=\frac{(\sqrt{5}+1)^2}{4}\end{gathered}$ $\begin{aligned} & =4 \cos ^2 \frac{\pi}{5}=2\left(1+\cos \frac{2 \pi}{p}\right) \equiv k\left(1+\cos \frac{2 \pi}{p}\right) \\ & \therefore \quad k+p=2+5=7 \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let circle $C_1: x^2+(y-4)^2=12$ intersects circle $C_2:(x-3)^2+y^2=13$ at $A$ and $B$. A quadrilateral $A C B D$ is formed by tangents at $A$ and $B$ to both circles. Find the diameter of circumcircle of quadrilateral $A C B D$.
questions.solution: Both circles are orthogonal to each other and $C(0,4)$ and $D(3,0)$ are centres and $C D$ will diameter of circumcircle $=\sqrt{3^2+4^2}=5$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Given a circle $(x+4)^2+(y-2)^2=25$. Another circle is drawn passing through $(-4,2)$ and touching the given circle internally at the point $\mathrm{A}(-4,7), \mathrm{AB}$ is a chord of length 8 units of the larger circle intersecting the other circle at the point C. Then AC will be _______
questions.solution: Let O be $(-4,2)$ OC is perpendicular to AC as OA is diameter $\Rightarrow C$ bisects chord $A B$ As $O$ is centre of bigger circle $\Rightarrow A C=4 \text { units }$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A circle $C$ is tangent to the $x$ and $y$-axis in first quadrant at the points $P$ and $Q$ respectively. $B C$ and $A D$ are parallel tangents to the circle with slope -1 . If the points $A$ and $B$ are on the $y$-axis while $C$ and $D$ are on x -axis and area of figure $A B C D$ is $900 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{sq}$. units. The radius of the circle is k then $\frac{\mathrm{k}}{3}$ is equal to $\qquad$
questions.solution:
$A B C D$ is a traperium and its area $=\frac{1}{2}(a+b) h$
Where $\frac{a+b}{2}=E F$ (mid parallel)
Arca $=2 \mathrm{r}(\mathrm{EF})$
Now equation of EF is $y=-x+c$....(1)
From equation (1) passes through ( $r . r$ )
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \mathrm{C}=2 \mathrm{r} \\ & \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}=2 \mathrm{r} \text {, hence } E=(2 r, 0) \text { and } F(0,2 r) \\ & \therefore E F=\sqrt{4 r^2+4 r^2}=2 \sqrt{2} r \\ & \therefore \text { Area } A B C D=2 r \cdot 2 \sqrt{2} r=4 \sqrt{2} r^2 \\ & \therefore 4 \sqrt{2} r^2=900 \sqrt{2}, r^2=225 \Rightarrow r=15\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The centres of two circles $C_1$ and $C_2$ each of unit radius are at a distance of 6 unit from each other. Let $P$ be the mid point of the line segment joining the centres of $C_1$ and $C_2$ and $C$ be a circle touching $C_1$ and $C_1$, then the radius of circle $C$ is
questions.solution: Let $A_1, A_2$ and $M$ be centres of circle $C_1, C_2$ and $C$ respectively the common tangent $P$ to $C_1$ and $C$ touch $C_1$ at $B_1, C$ at $B_2$ and $C_2$ also at $B_2$. From right angle $\triangle A_1 B_1 P$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { if } \angle A_1 P B_1=\alpha, \\ & \sin \alpha=\frac{A_1 B_1}{A_1 P}=\frac{1}{3} \\ & \cos \alpha=\frac{2 \sqrt{2}}{3} \\ & P B_1=2 \sqrt{2}=P B_2 \end{aligned}$ from $\triangle \mathrm{MPB}_2$ $\begin{aligned} & \tan \alpha=\frac{\mathrm{PB}_2}{\mathrm{MB}_2}=\frac{2 \sqrt{2}}{\mathrm{r}} \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2}}=\frac{2 \sqrt{2}}{r} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{r}=8 \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}), \mathrm{Q}(\mathrm{c}, \mathrm{d}) \& \mathrm{R}(\mathrm{e}, f)$ are three points satisfying the inequality $\mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{y}^2-6 \mathrm{x}-8 \mathrm{y} \lt 0$, where $\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{c}, \mathrm{d}, \mathrm{e}, \& f \in \mathrm{I}$, such that P is situated at least distance while $\mathrm{Q} \& \mathrm{R}$ are situated at greatest distance from the point $A(-2,4)$. Now internal bisector of angle $P$ of triangle $P Q R$ intersects the tangents to the circle $x^2+y^2-6 x-8 y=0$ drawn at origin and $\left(\frac{c+e}{2}+1, b\right)$. If area of the triangle formed by these three lines (two tangents and internal bisector of angle P ) is $\Delta$, then find the value of $3 \Delta$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & x^2+y^2-6 x-8 y \lt 0 \\ & (x-3)^2+(y-4)^2-25 \lt 0 \end{aligned}$ Point atleast distance from $(-2,4) \text { is } \mathrm{P}(\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{~b}) \equiv \mathrm{P}(-1,4)$ Points which are greatest distance from $(-2,4)$ are $\mathrm{Q}(\mathrm{c}, \mathrm{d})$ $\& \mathrm{R}(\mathrm{e}, f) \equiv \mathrm{Q}(7,6) \& \mathrm{R}(7,2)$ $\triangle \mathrm{PQR}$ is an isosceles triangle \& internal bisector of $\angle \mathrm{P}$ is $\mathrm{y}=4$ Equation of tangent at origin is $3 x+4 y=0$ equation of tangent at $\left(\frac{c+e}{2}+1, b\right) \equiv(8,4)$ is $\mathrm{x}=8$ Area of the right angled triangle formed by above three lines is $\Delta=\frac{1}{2} \times 10 \times \frac{40}{3}=\frac{200}{3}$ $\therefore \quad 3 \Delta=200$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Six points $\left(x_i, y_i\right), i=1,2, \ldots, 6$ are taken on the circle $x^2+y^2=4$ such that $\sum_{i=1}^6 x_i=8$ and $\sum_{i=1}^6 y_i=4$. The line segment joining orthocentre of a triangle made by any three points and the centroid of the triangle made by other three points passes through a fixed points $(\mathrm{h}, \mathrm{k})$, then $\mathrm{h}+\mathrm{k}$ is $\qquad$
questions.solution:
Let $\sum_{i=1}^6 x_i=\alpha$ and $\sum_{i=1}^6 y_i=\beta$
Let O be the orthocentre of the triangle made by ( $\left.\mathrm{x}_1, \mathrm{y}_1\right)$, ( $\mathrm{x}_2, \mathrm{y}_2$ ) and ( $\left.\mathrm{x}_3, \mathrm{y}_3\right)$ $\Rightarrow O$ is $\left(x_1+x_2+x_3 \cdot y_1+y_2+y_3\right) \equiv\left(\alpha_1 \cdot \beta_1\right)$
Similarly let G be the centroid of the triangle made by other three points $\Rightarrow G$ is $\left(\frac{x_4+x_5+x_6}{3}, \frac{y_4+y_5+y_6}{3}\right) \Rightarrow G$ is $\left(\frac{\alpha-\mu_1}{3}, \frac{\beta_1-\beta_1}{3}\right)$
The point dividing $O G$ in the ratio $3: 1$ is $\left(\frac{\alpha}{4}, \frac{\beta}{4}\right) \equiv(2,1) \Rightarrow h+k=3$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A straight line $L$ with the slope $\frac{3}{4}$ touches a circle whose radius is 5 units and centre lies on the $x$-axis. If the length of this tangent from a point on the $x$-axis is $\frac{p}{q}$ where $p, q \in N$, then find the least value of $(p+q)$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} l & =\sqrt{\left(\lambda+\frac{4 c}{3}\right)^2-25}=\sqrt{\left(\frac{3 \lambda+4 c}{3}\right)^2-25} \\ & =\sqrt{\left(\frac{25}{3}\right)^2-25} \\ & =\frac{20}{3} \equiv \frac{p}{q} \\ \therefore p+q & =23\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Three circles lie on a plane so that each of them externally touches the other two. Two of them has radius 3 , the third having radius unity. If $\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}$ and C are the points of tangency of those circles and the area of the triangle $A B C$ is $\frac{a \sqrt{b}}{c}$ then value of $c-a-b$ is, where $\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}$ and c are positive integers
questions.solution: $\cos \theta=\frac{3}{4}=\frac{x}{1}$ $\Rightarrow x=\frac{3}{4} \Rightarrow A B=\frac{3}{2}$ $p=\sqrt{4^2-3^2}=\sqrt{7}$ and $c_3 M=\frac{\sqrt{7}}{4}$ $\Rightarrow C M=\sqrt{7}-\frac{\sqrt{7}}{4}=\frac{3 \sqrt{7}}{4}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $S_1: x^2+y^2=4$ and $S_2: x^2+y^2-2 a x-2 b y+2=0$ touches each other, then 4 times of radius of the circle $S_2$ is:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & C_1 C_2=r_1+r_2 \\ & \sqrt{a^2+b^2}=2 \pm \sqrt{a^2+b^2-2} \\ & a^2+b^2=4+a^2+b^2 \pm 4 \sqrt{a^2+b^2-2} \\ & 4 \sqrt{a^2+b^2-2}=2 \\ & \sqrt{a^2+b^2-2}=\frac{1}{2}=\frac{r}{2} \\ & \therefore \quad 4 r_2=2 \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Tangents drawn from $P(1,8)$ to the circle $x^2+y^2-6 x-4 y-11=0$ touches the circle at the points $A$ and $B$ respectively. If the radius of the circle which passes through the points of intersection of circles $x^2+y^2-2 x-6 y$
$+6=0$ and $x^2+y^2+2 x-6 y+6=0$ and intersects the circumcircle of the $\triangle P A B$ orthogonally is equal to $\frac{\sqrt{p}}{q}$ where $p, q \in N$, then find the minimum value of $(p+q)$.
questions.solution:
Equation of circle circumscribing $\triangle \mathrm{PAB}$ is $(x-1)(x-3)+(y-8)(y-2)=0$ $\Rightarrow x^2+y^2-4 x-10 y+19=0$ $\ldots(i)$ As circle (ii) is orthogonal to circle (i), so $-2\left(\frac{2 \lambda-2}{\lambda+1}\right)-5(-6)=19+6$
$\Rightarrow 2\left(\frac{2 \lambda-2}{\lambda+1}\right)=5 \Rightarrow 4 \lambda-4=5 \lambda+5 \Rightarrow \lambda=-9$ Hence required equation of circle is $x^2+y^2+\frac{5}{2} x-6 y+6=0$ $\ldots(ii)$ Radius $=\sqrt{\frac{25}{16}+9-6}=\frac{\sqrt{73}}{4}=\frac{\sqrt{\mathrm{p}}}{\mathrm{q}}$ $\therefore \quad p=73, q=4$ Hence minimum value of $(p+q)=73+4=77$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $\tau$ be a circle with centre $C(3,5)$ and $P A$ and $P B$ are pair of tangents drawn from an external point $P(9,11)$ to the circle $\tau$. Find the distance between the origin and the point inside the quadrilateral $A C B P$ which is equidistant from its four vertices.
questions.solution: The point inside the quadrilateral $A C B P$ which is equidistant from all the four vertices is the centre $M(6,8)$ of the circle described on $P C$ as diameter. Hence, distance from origin to the point $M$ is $\sqrt{36+64}=\sqrt{100}=10$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider the circle $W \equiv x^2+y^2=81$. Let $A B$ be a diameter of circle $W . A B$ is extended through $A$ to $C$. Point $T$ lies on $W$ so that line $C T$ is tangent to $W$. Point $P$ is the foot of the perpendicular from $A$ to the line $C T$. Find the maximum value of (BP) ${ }^2$
questions.solution:
We have $(B P)^2=(B Q)^2+(Q P)^2=(O T+B M)^2+(O M+A R)^2$ $\begin{aligned} & =9^2(1+\sin \theta)^2+(9 \cos \theta+9 \cos \theta)^2=9^2(1+\sin \theta)^2+9^2 \cdot 4(\cos \theta)^2 \\ & =9^2\left[1+\sin ^2 \theta+2 \sin \theta+4 \cos ^2 \theta\right]=9^2\left[1+\sin ^2 \theta+2 \sin \theta+4-4 \sin ^2 \theta\right] \\ & =9^2\left[5+2 \sin \theta-3 \sin ^2 \theta\right]\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & =-(81)(3)\left[\sin ^2 \theta-\frac{2}{3} \sin \theta-\frac{5}{3}\right]=-(81)(3)\left[\left(\sin \theta-\frac{1}{3}\right)^2-\frac{1}{9}-\frac{15}{9}\right] \\ & =-(81)(3)\left[\left(\sin \theta-\frac{1}{3}\right)^2-\frac{16}{9}\right]=(81)(3)\left[\frac{16}{9}-\left(\sin \theta-\frac{1}{3}\right)^2\right]\end{aligned}$ For maximum value of $(B P)^2$, we must have $\sin \theta=\frac{1}{3}$
Hence $\left.(B P)^2\right|_{\max }=(81)(3)\left(\frac{16}{9}\right)=(27)(16)=432$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $p_1$ and $p_2$ are the two values of $p$ for which two perpendicular tangents can be drawn from the origin to the circle $x^2-6 x+y^2-2 p y+17=0$, then find the value of $\left(p_1^2+p_2^2\right)$
questions.solution:
The equation of given circle is
$\begin{aligned}& S(x, y)=x^2+y^2-6 x-2 p y+17=0 \Rightarrow(x-3)^2+(y-p)^2=\left(p^2-8\right) \\& S(0,0)=17\gt0\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad(0,0)$ lies outside the circle.
Equation of director circle of $S=0$ will be $(x-3)^2+(y-p)^2=2\left(p^2-8\right)$.
$\because$ Tangents drawn from $(0,0)$ to $S=0$ are perpendicular to each other
$\therefore \quad(0,0)$ must lie on director circle.
$\begin{aligned}& \therefore(0-3)^2+(0-p)^2=2\left(p^2-8\right) \\& \Rightarrow p^2=25 \Rightarrow p= \pm 5\end{aligned}$
Hence $\mathrm{p}_1^2+\mathrm{p}_2^2=(5)^2+(-5)^2=25+25=50$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
